description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for comparing 4-digit numbers

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent prerequisite knowledge, can focus on practice and applications

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete topic coverage

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps, needs prerequisite review before main content

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Greater Than and Less Than Symbols
Start main topic: Comparison strategy for 4-digit numbers

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review before attempting main topic

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Place Value in 4-digit Numbers - Quick Review

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you're ready to continue with the full topic.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

symbols_gt_lt.conceptName: Greater than and less than symbols

ordering_numbers.conceptName: Ordering numbers from least to greatest

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: Which number is greater: 4,567 or 4,756?
questions.explanation: Compare thousands: 4 = 4 (equal). Compare hundreds: 5 < 7. So 4,756 is greater.

questions.question: Which symbol correctly completes: 3,245 ___ 3,254?
questions.explanation: Compare thousands: 3 = 3. Compare hundreds: 2 = 2. Compare tens: 4 < 5. So 3,245 < 3,254.

ifAllCorrect.reason: Student demonstrated mastery of comparison and symbols, skip symbol review

ifAllWrong.reason: Student still struggling with basic comparison strategy

questions.question: Arrange in ascending order: 5,234, 5,342, 5,243
questions.explanation: All have 5 thousands. Compare hundreds: all have 2 or 3. 234 and 243 both have 2 hundreds. Compare tens in 234 vs 243: 3 < 4, so 234 < 243. Then 243 < 342. Order: 5,234, 5,243, 5,342.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for advanced challenge problems

ifWrong.message: Practice ordering more numbers before moving to applications

skip_ahead.action: Skip remaining basic videos, jump to application/challenge videos

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review videos based on error analysis

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before proceeding

high_score.message: Excellent! You have strong prerequisite knowledge. We've created a fast-track playlist focusing on comparing 4-digit numbers with practice and challenges.

good_score.message: Great work! You're ready to learn comparing and ordering 4-digit numbers. Your playlist covers all important concepts.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in prerequisite knowledge. Don't worry! We'll start with review videos on place value and comparing 3-digit numbers, then move to the new content.

low_score.message: You need to review prerequisite concepts first. We've created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation in place value and basic comparison. After watching, you can retake the quiz.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

conceptRetention.description: % of students who can correctly apply comparison strategy 1 week later

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 30-35 minutes with 85-95% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 75-85% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-60 minutes with 65-75% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (40 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 65-75 total minutes, expected 55-70% on practice quiz
